original_description: Begins a new piece of work in the ai-agent-skills repository. Two modes: EXISTING ISSUE (user provides a GitHub issue number or URL — fetches it, cuts the correct branch) and NEW ISSUE (user describes what needs doing — classifies as feature or bug, drafts a structured requirement using the repo's issue template, confirms with the user, creates the GitHub issue, then cuts the branch). Always checks GitHub auth first and confirms user identity. Use when the user says "start work on #14", "begin issue", "start a new feature", "I need to build X", "create an issue for Y", or passes a GitHub issue URL. Load the `conventions` skill for branch naming rules.

train_results.query: I want to work on this: https://github.com/psenger/ai-agent-skills/issues/5
I need to build a new skill for summarising Slack threads
create an issue for adding dark mode support to the vault-scribe skill
start work on the handoff skill bug where resume doesn't load constraints
I need to create a github issue and start working on it — the release skill is broken on main
I need to start a new feature — users should be able to export reports to PDF
start work on #14
begin issue 23
start a new feature branch for the design-critique skill enhancement
create a PR for this branch
cut a release
file a feature request for TypeScript type checking support in skills
commit these changes with a conventional commit message
ship this feature
list the open issues in this repo
what's the branch naming convention?
push my changes to origin
write a readme for this new skill
merge this PR
what was the last commit on main?
